meta.title: Sharps & Needlestick Management — Phlebotomy Round, Day-Ward 4B
meta.subtitle: Sharps-injury prevention + bloodborne-pathogen exposure control plan (OSHA 1910.1030 / EU 2010/32)
meta.author: HSE Healthcare
meta.deid_notice: All personnel and patients are referred to by role label. The source patient is referenced by role only; the injured worker's identity and post-exposure (PEP) medical record are held confidentially and separately per OSHA 1910.1030(f). The Sharps Injury Log below is de-identified and aggregated, with categories of fewer than five suppressed. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting and the re-identification / exposure key is held separately, not part of this document.

sections.text: Phlebotomy round, Day-Ward 4B. Sharps in use: hollow-bore venepuncture needles, winged blood-collection sets, IV cannulae, and lancets. The elimination/substitution decision (below) was recorded BEFORE any device or PPE. Source-patient identity/serostatus and the injured worker's PEP record were scrubbed to role level before drafting.
Hierarchy-ranked controls (eliminate → safety-engineered devices → work practices → PPE/PEP last)

Engineering-first: unnecessary sharps are eliminated first (needle-free IV connectors), then the residual sharps are controlled by safety-engineered devices and point-of-use disposal. A 'staff to take care / wear gloves' treatment of a sharp that could be eliminated or engineered out is a FLAG pushed up the hierarchy — PPE and PEP are the documented last lines, never the headline control.

rows.control: Eliminate unnecessary sharps — needle-free connectors on IV lines; ban recapping by hand
Safety-engineered venepuncture needles + winged sets with integrated sharps-injury protection (residual sharps)
Point-of-use sharps containers at every trolley (fill line marked); no-recapping rule + single-handed scoop where re-sheathing is by design
Gloves / eye protection as the residual barrier + the confidential PEP pathway

sections.text: PPE and PEP are NOT the primary control. A plan whose headline control is 'staff to take care / wear gloves' with no elimination or safety-engineered-device step, or that permits recapping by hand, is a FLAG pushed up the hierarchy.

rows: Selected by the phlebotomy team trial (non-managerial frontline input)
Frontline evaluation; needle-free connectors substituted on lines
Non-engineered alternative withdrawn (no recorded justification to retain)
sections.caption: Annual safer-device evaluation with non-managerial frontline-worker input (OSHA 1910.1030(c)(1)(iv) / Needlestick Act)

sections.text: Sharps Injury Log structure (de-identified / aggregated — OSHA 1910.1030(h)(5))

sections.caption: Structural / aggregated log only — never line-level identified; categories of fewer than five suppressed so a suppressed cell cannot be back-calculated from totals

sections.text: Confidential pathway — the source patient is referenced by role only, source-patient testing is performed WITH CONSENT, and the injured worker's exposure/PEP medical record is held confidentially and separately from this plan (OSHA 1910.1030(f)). No source patient or worker is named in this circulated artifact.

items: Immediate first aid — encourage bleeding, wash with soap and water, irrigate mucous membranes / eyes.
Confidential incident report — logged to the de-identified Sharps Injury Log; the worker is a role label.

Source-patient testing — with the source patient's CONSENT and confidentiality; the source patient is 'the source patient' by role only; the serostatus result is held in a separate confidential record, never in this plan.
PEP — HBV (vaccination status check + immunoglobulin/booster per protocol), HCV (baseline + follow-up testing), HIV (risk-assess + PEP started within the protocol window where indicated).
Follow-up — the confidential occupational-health follow-up schedule, held separately.
Hepatitis-B vaccination offered to all staff with occupational sharps exposure (OSHA 1910.1030(f)); uptake reported in aggregate only.
Individual vaccination / serostatus is special-category health data — held confidentially, never circulated in this plan.

rows.title: Behaviour-led control proposed where the sharp could be eliminated
rows.description: A draft control headlined 'staff to take care / wear gloves' was flagged and pushed up the hierarchy — unnecessary sharps are eliminated and the residual controlled by safety-engineered devices first.

rows.title: Non-engineered lancet retained without justification
rows.description: A non-engineered lancet was in use without a recorded justification; withdrawn in favour of a single-use auto-retract device.

sections.text: After elimination (needle-free IV connectors), safety-engineered devices, point-of-use disposal, and the no-recapping rule, the residual sharps-exposure risk is framed via risk_matrix as Low–Medium, with the confidential PEP pathway as the last line.

rows.action: Complete the changeover to safety-engineered venepuncture needles + winged sets across the round
Withdraw the non-engineered lancet; document the safer-device decision with frontline input
Run the annual safer-device evaluation with non-managerial frontline-worker input
Verify the confidential PEP pathway + HBV-vaccination offer are in place and tested

items: Sharps-injury counts taken from the de-identified Sharps Injury Log; categories of fewer than five are suppressed — a missing figure is a [GAP], never invented.
India site: state biomedical-waste sharps-segregation return owed — [GAP] (resolve the state via hse-india; BMW Rules 2016; no national form number minted).

OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1030 — (c) Exposure Control Plan + (c)(1)(iv) annual safer-device evaluation with frontline input, (d)(2) engineering & work-practice controls, (f) confidential post-exposure follow-up + HBV vaccination, (h)(5) Sharps Injury Log; the Needlestick Safety and Prevention Act (PL 106-430)
EU Council Directive 2010/32/EU + the UK Health and Safety (Sharp Instruments in Healthcare) Regulations 2013 with COSHH (cite the member-state transposition; RIDDOR for reportable exposures)
India Bio-Medical Waste Management Rules 2016 sharps segregation via hse-india (no national form number)
Review trigger: annual safer-device review / on-device-change / on-incident — whichever is first
Decision-support only — a competent person (occupational-health / infection-control professional) must review before use.
